transcript.text: One second for us all.
I was swapping over my headphones.
Give me a quick call on yourself.
Oh, good.
How are things?
Good.
Sorry about the confusion there.
Yes, I can hear you.
Do you hear me now?
Yeah, yeah.
Perfect.
How's it going?
Good.

Sorry, Alex wanted to try to join us, but he's on the road today, so I figured it'd be good for us to have the conversation, and then we can always loop him in for a conversation when he's back in the Bay.
How has your summer been?
Really good.
Yeah, things are crazy, crazy busy.
Not a lot of time off, as you can imagine, but...
I would expect that.
Yeah.
Yeah, well, if the testimonials on your website say anything, I don't think you've slept this summer.
Yeah, yeah, no, it's been wild, and I feel like as soon as August started, it was just like...
Everybody decided to want to spend money right away kind of thing, which is great, but also like where were everybody during the summer, you know, as well.
And so it's just crazy, a good July, great June, great July, insane August, essentially.
Yeah, that's awesome.
There has been a, I can even see it internally in terms of how we're thinking about our own AI tools and like starting to adopt.
I think there has been a recognition that it's not going to be as easy as click a button, download a software, and AI is going to be a magic trick that just like fixes your processes.
And I think, you know, you guys set an impressive spot in the market to say, we know that, you know, we're not going to promise out of the box, perfect magician tricks.
But there's a human, there's a human way to interact with these tools, and we want to support you on that journey.
I can only imagine how much more that story is resonating when folks are in the renewal cycle.
An AI tool that they haven't figured out 12 months in.
And I feel like that is the cycle that we hit starting in like June, July, August this year.
Yeah, yeah.
And I think AI Answers just started to pour gasoline on the fire, you know, so.
Sure.
It's true.
It's true.
So are you starting to think about the Series V?
How's the year shaping up?
so last week it really kind of picked up.
We were, so we've decided to kind of pull things in.
And so we have a couple of like partner meetings coming up this week, later this week.
But we've had like very small group of investors, like less than 10 that were like had just been talking to you in the last several months that reached back out.
so definitely starting to kind of pick things up.
And so, but we're hoping to run a really, really efficient process so that it's like we were not the main constraint and just been insane on the amount of demand, which is great.
But also like.
Yeah.
We got to get back to work too, you know?
So it's like, oh, it's a balancing act, you know?
You're running a company, you got to raise, but you also got to run the company.
can't just like, hey, company, I'm going to be away for a month talking to investors, you know?
That's just like, so we've got to do the process as efficient as possible, you know?
Yeah, 100%.
You're doing it yourself.
It sounds like you're not going to bring out a banker.
No, no, yeah, yeah.
It's going to take longer if they get involved in the reality.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
And one of our customers brought in, I mean, they were raising a massive amount, but they brought in a banker and they had a horrible experience as well.
You know, it is so, we sit on both sides, right?
And it is very hit or miss.
It's a relationship-driven industry in the same way investors are.
But their interest is to prolong it to get the highest price, right?
And so, like, if you're looking to do something fast and clean, that's not always their preferred route.
It can definitely, we see it on both ends, definitely cause some tension.
What's the product update?
So when we last spoke, was June or so, the product was very much under the hood.

And I would love to hear a little bit about the evolution, because one thing that we see a lot right now are tools that claim to deliver a lot of AI value, but under the hood are actually quite unimpressive.
And it's unusual to see a tool like GrowthX, it's the opposite, where under the hood, there's a lot of really complicated stuff going on, but the UI is pretty simple.
It's just meant to be sufficient to get you to the actual value.
So I'd love to understand a little bit more about how that, like the productization of the company is evolving as more folks get to be using it.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Like, so the way we kind of think about it is the, so the first sales call is happening in AI Answers now almost.
Almost.
And so people are spending so much more time in AI answers than anyone thought.
And so in a lot of ways, if they're spending 15, 20 minutes, they are almost ingesting that information as if it was like an expert word of mouth.
And whereas I think initially a lot of people just thought like, hey, you know, chat TPT is just like high level and then they're going to go to your website.
I think more and more that's shifting where more of that time is spent prior to going to a brand's website.
And by the time they do, they kind of have a very short list already and a lot of information that they've already consumed.

And so now you have to be the most comprehensive resource across every possible thing that your audience cares about and might ask and be the best answer in order to be part of the answer to even be within consideration.
Right.
And so I think that switched kind of people's mentality from AI to scale, you know, crappy content to we need quality content.
But now volume and speed is no longer optional because if you have a thousand.
Pages on your site, you can't refresh those thousand pages once a decade anymore.
so I think kind of part of that, you know, and so it's like, I think a lot of what we do is like, there's a lot of these artifacts that we create for companies.

And then that kind of help give like the big picture of what those, the personas are, like their frustrations and everything that can like kind of scale quality, you know, and, and then just kind of summarize it a little bit, like we have these workflows that, that will generate the content and you have an editor that guides that process along.
And so for instance, like something like this, where it's doing research on 13 best coding AI, coding agents for complex code bases, right?
Like, like, so it's doing very complex and detailed research on the topic.
But then an editor can actually like, um, yeah.
Kind of guide the process along, you know, and you can see like there's even like very detailed tables and things like that.
But then we also build a context aware agent assistant that kind of can ingest all this artifacts and you can, you know, kind of select anything here and, you know, bullets or something like that.
Yeah, like very simple kind of like commands just start to like essentially use the models, but also be able to edit in line and accept any changes, you know.
And so you can see here, okay, I said edit bullets and then edit like bullets immediately within the right context, essentially, you know.
To make sure I understand how Fathom works, you are looking to make a new article of quality content on the website.
And so you're going to tell the platform, I would like to make like the assignment brief, the aspect of the lab.
I would like to make this article, it's what I'm looking for.
It's going to go to a little bit.
It's going to make an outline.
At some point in that process, I might jump in and say, oh, that outline looks good, but we actually want to focus on this.
It might go back and iterate.
Then it's going to draft me up the article.
Now as an editor, I get to jump back in and say, oh, this is unimportant.
This is important to add bullets, take out whatever it might be, add in a quote, something like that.
It looks like there's some fact-checking, obviously really important for quality content.
Then I'm ready to go.
The content is publishable.
It's updated.
It's set to be up my website.
It took only, looks like, less than 20 minutes if you add up all those little timestamps, whereas a human would have previously had to do whatever, an hour or two of research, go on and write this.

But it's the same person engaging with the platform as would have previously done all that research because you still leave the knowledge base that person would have had if they had been the one to have originally written it.
difference is we're delivering this as a service, and so we're mapping out what the different...
Personas and audiences.
So just to give you like a little bit of a visual.
So we go through like discovery to create these artifacts.
Like we go, we define the personas.
We map out what writing guidelines, what great looks like.
And then we map out every topic that those personas will care about.
And that's also what we're tracking in terms of AI visibility, you know, and so we're executing against an inventory of different opportunities that we've, which is essentially the content strategy.
That we're shaping for them.
And then we're executing.
So for instance, this one for Augment Code, we published and like all the way to the end.
And then now, like, if you go to like fast AI coding tools for enterprises, they're the number one before they didn't even show up.
Right.
So the reason, say, Augment Code does this is to actually maybe to go back.
We have been spending a lot of time on how SEO has changed because...
If you look at like, where does chat CPT pull data from?
It's like 40 some percent of their information comes from Reddit.
And that's not a traditional avenue, right?
That most folks are putting quality content on Reddit is a little bit ironic, I think, for those of us who grew up with Reddit being in front of the internet.
In the use case, for most customers, we aren't showing up on the preferred search vendors, which are Perplexity or chat CPT or Gemini.
And in order to be identifiable to customers, we need to make content that shows up on those places, and we need to generate it efficiently.
And that's where growthx becomes valuable?
Yes and no.
I think content, so the way to think about it, content is the atomic unit of marketing in a lot of ways.
And outcomes are the atomic value unit for marketing, right?
And so, like...

What we're trying to do, even if AI answers weren't a thing, would still be relevant, which is creating an engine that helps people create credible, comprehensive, up-to-date content that builds trust with their audience and gains credibility.
That then, based on that, they can turn that into distribution and then eventually conversions.

It's just that the difference is that AI answers a new distribution channel that just happens to be sucking up a lot of people's time and driving a lot of how they think about trust, who they trust, who they look for information about.
And so, whereas before, if their organic traffic wasn't doing very well, it wasn't like my CEO is telling me to go fix this.
Whereas right now, if they're not showing up in AI answers, it's like, holy crap.
Like, I'll give you an example.
We just kicked off with a customer, relayfi.com today, and they have 313 pages.
Published over the last three years.
They haven't done almost anything in the last six months.
But then out of nowhere, even though they knew that was an area they should have focused more on lately, their LLM referrals started going through the roof earlier this year.
And that's just from outdated content.
So they realized, holy crap, there's a lot of value here.
And then in the last six months, it plateaued.
And it's still driving some, but they're like, whoa, there's a lot of opportunity here.
So organic traffic is down, but still generating some things, even though we haven't touched a content in three years, essentially.
LLM referrals grew like crazy without us doing anything.
We should go invest in this.
And so they brought us in.
So that's a good, but also curious on kind of, I think the main thing is mostly like kind of how far along I think conversations are too.
Just want to be kind of like mindful of kind of how you guys are thinking about things as well.
And just like also be respectful of your time as well, of just making sure.
Like

Yeah, because of how far along kind of conversations are with everyone already, you know, yeah, and how you guys are thinking about the space and, you know, and, and if it makes sense, you know, yeah, it, I didn't understand from our last conversation, how much the drive may be around some of the AI answers, LLM referrals, because we've, yeah, I think that's a category we've spent a lot of time on.
So we've been looking at a lot of real-time data access companies that are trying to re, kind of refurbish their SEO practices.

We've looked at a lot of ways that businesses are tagging or not tagging things internally to try to show up and these LLM answers, and it's, it's really hard to figure out why you show up and why you don't.

It sounds like, really, like I was like, why did this old piece of content pick up traction, and they don't really know, and I think we see it internally to our portfolio, too, that you want to put out quality, quality work.
We're all seeing a lot of, a lot of folks, maybe a little disillusioned with how ChatGPT fits in the workflow.
And so we've been pretty keen on companies that could crack the workflow, like almost like a Notion plus ChatGPT plus some industry-specific relevant information has been really interesting to us.
Yeah, yeah.
And that's very similar to kind of how we approach it and what we built.
I think the difference is like, even if you have the best tools, if you don't deliver as a service now, like people are still going to be successful.
Yeah, they're still not going to use it.
To it, you know, it's kind of like trying to use ChatGPT to do an M&A deal.
It's like, sure, it can write you a lot of the docs.
But it doesn't really have a setup that's an ideal format.
And so you're doing a lot of work that at some point I think folks will say, I just do this myself.
I don't need to try to work.
Could you remind me high level where the business is this year?
Because I think I mentioned last time that Summit tends to come in at that 10 to 15 million ARR range.
And I know that was the kind of 2025 goals.
But if you're not quite at that point.
Then I candidly think it's probably early for us from this year.
Yeah, yeah, that makes sense.
Yeah, we just crossed 11 million in run rate.
It will probably finish the month at like closer to like 12, most likely 11.5 to 12.
We just kicked off already booked, but not yet.
Kicked off, if you will.
And then we're projecting 15 to 16 at the end of the year and then 45 to 50 next year.
And then we're just closing the books in August.
But, or Magic, and I can send you like an investment memo as well that has some more details.
We just don't have the financials on this one.
There's another one that as we get closer that we share, like we have a data room already with several investors.
But so I think like if we get a little bit closer, we can definitely share a lot more.
But yeah, like I think overall, like we're a little bit over 70% gross margin, burn multiple of about 0.5.
A 15 plus like magic number, which essentially means we need to invest more in and go to market.
You know, we're highly efficient.
We're burning too efficient.
Yeah, exactly.
A little too, too efficient.

And then we're also launching a self-serve AI visibility tool that won't compete with the service, but will be a loss leader as almost like a front end offer to the marketer on the access to that data.

So if you think about like similar web or Crunchbase, help Crunchbase democratize information about startups and companies, like kind of like the same idea for AI visibility that we're launching in probably two weeks, three weeks.
And so, yeah, like we're not the main constraint.
So right now it's mostly like we're want to double down on the platform, really invest heavily and go to market and go like it's a $500 billion service market that.
Yeah.
That doesn't even tap, that's not even counting like the, any kind of softer self-serve aspects of the visibility side.
And I think people forget like, you know, like SEMrush, for example, is the biggest SEO tool in the market.
There's a billion market cap.
Services is 500 billion, right?
Like there's 8,000 agencies in the U.S.
that only focus on content and SEO alone.
Like, you know, it's like, it's up for grabs essentially.
And the winner is not be a tool that you hit play and call it a date.
Like Sentinel One, one of our customers, is not going to let a cheap SEO tool hit publish.
They're certainly not going to, you know, there's so much nuance.
It's way too important to these businesses and what content represents them out there, 100%.
Well, so, okay.
So it sounds like you're kind of far along the lines.
What would, if this is an interesting fit, what would you, what would you recommend us do?
I think, look, the numbers, high level numbers and what we high level know about the business sounds super interesting.
Of course, there's always lots.
There's always lots more to learn, right, is digging into a company.
So if you're open to us engaging on it, we would love to learn more.
We're in the Bay.
So I think you're in San Francisco, aren't you?
Yeah, yeah, we're in South Park, right by the Blue Bottle.
Okay, yeah.
So we're in Menlo Park.
So it's not hard to coordinate.
Alex is in New York for the next few weeks.
But it's not hard to coordinate things in person if we wanted to move relatively fast.
Do you have a data room that we could have access to, run it through?
So maybe to tell you our process, and then you could have a sense of what we do.
Our first step is to sort of get the numbers high level and go to our IC and do what we would call preview.

And the hope there is if we don't have interest in moving forward because of whether it's financials or business model or whatever it might be, we don't want to waste any of your time doing 100 calls.
So we'll run it through our IC first.
And if it's like, yeah, this is pretty interesting, we would like to learn more, this is a potential.

Fathom, and the committee as a whole feels that way, then we would come say, hey, can we do a deep dive on the tech, know, bring in our people, like spend your time respectfully to do some more diligence.
So I would like to bring this to that stage.
And then I can give you some concrete, whether as a committee, we would move forward on it.
We don't have like a veto.
There's no veto kind of power in the committee.
It's just a majority decision for each of the steps gated from there on out.
So we try to be respectful of time by, you know, not moving through a hundred hoops until we have the broader support of the committee that will ultimately say, yeah, let's invest or not.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.

I think like the only thing to be mindful is we're on like fourth and fifth calls with like Iconic and a few others and we're talking to the entire partnership or managing partners already, you know, so like I just want to be mindful so that it's not like, you know, I think so.
I think best step here would be taking the investment memo with.
Which is what we share with a lot of partners already, you know, and kind of running any process on your end.
then I think at that point, if you feel like the process can be accelerated, it probably can make a lot of sense.
I think you all have a great portfolio, a lot of great reach and things like that.
You know, I think we're less than like trying to add more people to the process.
Like we've already had talked.
And so it's more of like, we're open to it, but not looking for it.
You've got a lot, you've got a lot of conversations on your plate.
I get it.
And so it's mostly kind of like this balancing act of like making sure like by the time, you know, it's like we had a investor reach out.
was like, hey, we can have our first meeting September 19th.
I was like, yeah, let's just talk after like our series.
Yeah, it's too late.
It's too late.
And it's completely okay.
So super, super flexible.
Yeah.
How much would you raise this round?
Yeah, we're raising 40 to 60 million right now.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay, perfect.
Well, then why don't I do this?
Why don't I go back to our committee, show them what we've got, let them know the context.
I can get back to you in like a day, maybe two.
We can do it over email, you know.
And I can give you a yes, no, move forward.
We can come up to see you in person in the city or you can come down here if that's the next step and we'll go from there.
Or if you end up going with other folks and, you know, becomes a series C, the 40 is probably the smaller side of the checks we'd write.
So it would be good to stay in touch either way.
And, you know, Iconic has a history of being one cycle ahead of the businesses that we really like a lot.
So that would be a pretty good outcome.
Yeah, we spent a lot of time with Doug Pepper and Ryan as well.
And I know they just, yeah.
Yeah.
So we're, they're a great team.
So, you know, I'm sorry, you've got great folks on the table.
So let's see if there's a way for us to accelerate our timeframe and make it make sense.
And if it just doesn't make sense, we'll stick around for a series C.
Yeah, that sounds great.
Awesome.
Perfect.
Well, we'll talk soon.
And then let me know, got the link, to the investment memo?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Do you mind sending it over email just so that it can be forwarded around to the team easily?
That would be great.
Yeah, that's perfect.
Sounds good.
Awesome.
Thanks, Marcel.
Appreciate the time, and we'll be in touch over the next 24 hours or so.
All right.
Take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss GrowthX's progress, product updates, and potential investment opportunity with Summit Partners.](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX has crossed $11M in run rate, projecting $15-16M by year-end and $45-50M next year](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=903.0)
  - [Currently raising $40-60M in Series B, with advanced discussions ongoing with other investors](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=903.0)
  - [Product evolution focuses on AI-driven content creation and AI visibility, addressing the changing landscape of SEO and content marketing](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
  - [Summit Partners interested but may be early in their typical investment cycle ($10-15M ARR range)](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX Business Update

  - [Run rate just crossed $11M, likely to finish month at $11.5-12M](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=905.0)
  - [Projecting $15-16M by year-end, $45-50M next year](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)
  - [70%+ gross margin, 0.5 burn multiple, 15+ magic number (indicating high efficiency)](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=952.0)
  - [Launching self-serve AI visibility tool as a loss leader in 2-3 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=975.0)

### Product Evolution and Market Positioning

  - [AI Answers becoming crucial for first sales interactions](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [Focus on comprehensive, quality content creation to build trust and credibility](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [New workflow: Research → Outline → Draft → Edit → Fact-check → Publish](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)
  - [Service-based approach, not just a tool, to ensure successful implementation](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=533.0)
  - [Addressing $500B service market, with potential to disrupt traditional content/SEO agencies](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1036.0)

### Fundraising Process

  - [Raising $40-60M in Series B](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1258.0)
  - [Advanced discussions with several investors, including Iconic](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=150.0)
  - [Running an efficient process to balance fundraising with company operations](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=165.0)
  - [No banker involved, managing the process internally](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)

### Summit Partners' Interest and Next Steps

  - [Summit typically invests at $10-15M ARR range, making GrowthX potentially early](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=887.0)
  - [Sydney to present to Summit's IC for initial interest assessment](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1116.0)
  - [If positive, potential for accelerated process and in-person meetings](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1136.0)
  - [Alternative: Stay in touch for potential Series C involvement](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1280.0)

### Market Trends in AI and Content Marketing

  - [Shift in content consumption patterns due to AI tools like ChatGPT](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=597.0)
  - [Increasing importance of AI visibility and LLM referrals](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
  - [Need for businesses to adapt content strategies for AI-driven search and discovery](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Marcel to send investment memo to Sydney via email](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
  - [Sydney to present to Summit's IC within 24-48 hours](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1262.0)
  - [Sydney to follow up with Marcel on Summit's interest and potential next steps](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1267.0)
  - [If interested, schedule potential in-person meeting in San Francisco or Menlo Park](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1095.0)
  - [If timing doesn't align, maintain relationship for potential future involvement (e.g., Series C)](https://fathom.video/share/kekvjnyW7PpV-1ZQMdZEHH-HsuLxYysJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1287.0)

action_items.description: Present GrowthX info to Summit Partners IC for preview decision
Email Marcel w/ decision on moving forward within 1-2 days
Email investment memo to Sydney for forwarding to Summit Partners team
